repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: its overlap-hedge proposal does not give this selector-label parity-model compatibility construction.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic, Theorem 5 proof: already adapts the Shpitser--Pearl bit-parity witness to CS-SCM serious-selector semantics.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: no local two-block CS-hedge with a proposed context-marginal coupling-invariance theorem.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeCorreaBareinboim2020General gives hedgelets/thickets for arbitrary supplied experimental laws, while LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic supplies selector-aware parity witnesses for covered failures; neither states this exact criterion.
ShpitserPearl2006Joint: rooted C-forest hedges and binary parity countermodels already witness ordinary interventional nonidentification.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: no characterization of the remaining systematic-selection terminal branch by a context-specific hedge.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic, Algorithm 1--2 and Theorem 5: the paper proves nonidentification for three named failure returns, says it covers all but one failure case, and conjectures completeness.
LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic proves SS-ID soundness and three failure converses; conditional closure after resolving its remaining failure branch is routine.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The ordinary rooted-forest parity witness is already the classical ShpitserPearl2006Joint result, so it can only remain a sanity reduction.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship-facing comparison gives only LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic by bibkey and an internal cs_fail_2 tag; anchor it to the uncovered Algorithm-2 terminal return and distinguish it from Theorem 5's covered Algorithm-2 line-3 hedge failure.

soundness_flags.label: ass:finite-binary-cs-semantics
soundness_flags.one_line: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic defines selector components with binary seriousness indicators but does not make finite binary observed responses the standard CS-SCM semantics; retag this as the constructed finite-binary submodel.

soundness_flags.label: ass:label-assignment-coherence
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited CS-SCM definition has one selector component per child and does not establish this many-label simultaneous-assignment condition as a standard named assumption; present it as a new encoding condition and justify its correspondence.

soundness_flags.label: def:context-restriction / def:parity-cs-scm / Lemma 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Nothing requires a label on an edge to target that edge's child, so rho_u can delete an arbitrary parental or bidirected contribution while the alleged serious assignment is to an unrelated W_ell; this is not the cited CS-SCM selector semantics and the compatibility lemma is not determined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Failure of a T_L- or T_R-to-root path does not hide B_L or B_R from the supplied full law: unassigned vertices in both blocks remain observed and their joint XOR response generally retains a nonzero kappa Fourier coefficient, so contextwise equality does not follow.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated exposure predicate does not force c_{y^star}>0: treatment or response constants can flip its sign, and additional directed paths from other parity-injected block vertices can cancel the B_L+B_R coefficient.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 1
soundness_flags.one_line: F_1 is never required to be an R-rooted C-forest, while an ordinary hedge requires both forest members to be such; moreover the proposed empty-context severing condition conflicts with the CS-hedge requirement that both empty-context connections hold.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: honest_scope / Theorem 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The intended completeness claim is restricted to finite binary CS-SCM countermodels with positive supplied selector contexts, not arbitrary alphabets or positivity regimes.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any repaired frontier is completeness only on the explicitly defined finite-binary positive-context model class.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the arbitrary lambda map by an explicit child-indexed selector encoding and prove that every rho_u deletion is the latent-projection consequence of a serious assignment to that child.

Rebuild the marginal-invariance theorem with a condition that every supplied context removes or fixes an entire parity block from the observed law, then give a smallest labelled ADMG calculation verifying P_{u,1/2}=P_{u,-1/2} and Q_{1/2}!=Q_{-1/2}.
State the ordinary-hedge reduction using a pair (F_0,F_1) explicitly required to be rooted C-forests, with empty context added as a supplied law and no contradictory severing condition.
Name the exact uncovered SS-ID return by Algorithm-2 line and predicate, separating it from the already-covered line-3 hedge failure before retaining the completeness headline.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a substantive semantic rebuild of the selector/edge correspondence and parity-invariance kernel; the present load-bearing sanity failures require rejection rather than downstream derivation.
